# Rio On Pools

Rio On Pools is a licensed tour operator in the Cayo District, Belize, offering guided tours to
Rio On Pools, a series of natural swimming pools carved into exposed granite bedrock inside
Mountain Pine Ridge Forest Reserve.

Contact: info@rioonpools.com, +501 639-0074. Website: https://rioonpools.com

## Tours

1. Rio On Pools & Mountain Pine Ridge Half-Day Tour - $95 per person, 5-6 hours. Visits Rio On
   Pools (swim), Rio Frio Cave (walk-through limestone cavern), Big Rock Falls (150 ft waterfall
   with swimming hole), and a Pine Ridge viewpoint. Includes licensed guide, round trip
   transportation from San Ignacio, park access, and bottled water.

2. Caracol & Rio On Pools Full-Day Combo - $250 per person, 2 person minimum, 10-11 hours.
   Visits Caracol Maya ruins (the largest ancient Maya city found in Belize, including Caana,
   the tallest man-made structure in the country at about 43 meters), a Pine Ridge viewpoint, and
   a swim at Rio On Pools on the return leg. Includes licensed guide, round trip transportation,
   Caracol entrance fee, packed lunch, and bottled water.

## Key facts

- Rio On Pools sits inside Mountain Pine Ridge Forest Reserve, established 1944, covering roughly
  43,333 hectares, one of Belize's oldest protected areas.
- Rio On Pools is carved into granite from the ancient Maya Mountains batholith, geologically
  distinct from the limestone that covers most of Belize.
- No separate entrance fee for Rio On Pools; reserve access is free with sign-in at the gatehouse.
- Best time to visit is the dry season, December through May, especially February to April.
- Swimming carries real risk from currents, slippery granite, and deep spots; guided visits with
  life vests for weaker swimmers are recommended.
- Booking is lead-capture only; no online payment. Submit a request and the team confirms
  availability and payment within 24 hours.
